query: What do MESSENGER MDIS images show about the relationship between Mercury's north polar low reflectance deposits and permanently shadowed terrain?

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS BDR Data Archive
candidates.reasoning: Curated shortlist (PDS4). Map-projected MDIS basemap (BDR/RDR) products suitable for mapping north polar surface units and comparing mapped low-reflectance areas against terrain context. Stable bundle LIDVID: urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_4001::1.0. Access: https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MSGRMDS_4001/ (label: https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MSGRMDS_4001/bundle_messenger_mdis_4001.xml). Likely_related to PDS3 volume MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-BDR-V1.0 (same archive family/holdings name MSGRMDS_4001).

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS MAP PROJECTED BASEMAP ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Curated shortlist (PDS3). Legacy MDIS map-projected basemap archive (BDR/RDR) that underlies many Mercury mosaic products; useful for north polar mapping work. Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_4001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/). Likely_related to PDS4 bundle urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_4001::1.0.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS DEM Data Archive
candidates.reasoning: Curated shortlist (PDS4). MDIS-derived digital elevation models (DEMs) can be used to model illumination/permanent shadow for polar craters and to co-register MDIS reflectance products with topography. Stable bundle LIDVID: urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_dem_1001::1.0. Access: https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MESSDEM_1001/ (label: https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MESSDEM_1001/bundle_messenger_mdis_dem_1001.xml). Likely_related to PDS3 volume MESS-H-MDIS-5-DEM-ELEVATION-V1.0.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS DIGITAL ELEVATION MODEL ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Curated shortlist (PDS3). Legacy MDIS DEM volume; supports terrain/illumination context for identifying permanently shadowed terrain (PSR) and comparing with mapped low-reflectance deposits. Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/mess-h-mdis-5-dem-elevation-v1.0/MESSDEM_1001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/). Likely_related to PDS4 bundle urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_dem_1001::1.0.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS CALIBRATED DATA ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Curated shortlist (PDS3). Calibrated MDIS images (CDR) provide the radiometrically corrected inputs needed to investigate reflectance variations near Mercury’s north pole; can be combined with geometry (DDR) and DEMs to compare low-reflectance deposits vs. permanently shadowed terrain. Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_2001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/). No corresponding PDS4 MDIS calibrated-image bundle was found in this bounded search.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS UNCALIBRATED DATA ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Additional candidate (PDS3). Uncalibrated MDIS images (EDR) if you need original acquisition pixels or to apply custom calibration/photometric correction before analyzing reflectance at high latitudes. Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_1001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/).

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS DERIVED DATA ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Additional candidate (PDS3). MDIS Derived Data Record (DDR) geometry (e.g., incidence/emission/phase angles, footprint metadata) for Mercury images—often required to distinguish true low reflectance from illumination effects when comparing deposits to permanently shadowed terrain boundaries. Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_3001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/).

candidates.title: MESS MDIS MAP PROJECTED REGIONAL TARGETED MOSAIC ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Additional candidate (PDS3). Regional targeted mosaics of MDIS WAC/NAC images; useful when your analysis focuses on specific north polar craters/regions rather than global basemaps. Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_8001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/).

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS MAP PROJECTED MULTISPECTRAL ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Additional candidate (PDS3). Map-projected multispectral WAC products that can help discriminate surface units and characterize spectral differences between low-reflectance deposits and surrounding terrain (outside/near PSRs). Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_5001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/).

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS 3-COLOR MAP PROJECTED MULTISPECTRAL ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Additional candidate (PDS3). 3-color map-projected multispectral MDIS products for enhanced-color context mapping near the north pole. Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_6001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/).

tool_input.summary:
These are the datasets that directly match your query based on the stated constraints (MESSENGER / MDIS imaging of Mercury, suitable for comparing north polar low-reflectance units with permanently shadowed terrain using map-projected products + geometry/topography)… and here are additional datasets that can also help answer the question.

1) Clarifying Questions: None.

2) Interpreted Scope
- Target/region: Mercury; emphasis on the north polar region.
- Phenomenon: low-reflectance deposits (LRD) vs permanently shadowed terrain (PSR). (Note: this run focuses on finding the relevant PDS imaging/topography datasets; it does not interpret what the images “show.”)
- Mission/instrument: MESSENGER / MDIS (WAC+NAC).
- Practical data needs inferred: map-projected mosaics/basemaps for unit mapping; calibrated images; geometry (incidence/emission/phase) to control for lighting; DEM/topography to support PSR/illumination modeling.

3) Search Plan (bounded)
- Catalog-first discovery with the PDS dataset catalog (pds_catalog_search_tool) for MESSENGER+MDIS datasets and for keywords like “north polar” / “permanently shadowed” / “low reflectance deposit.”
- PDS4 registry bundle discovery (pds4search_bundles_tool) to identify modern PDS4 bundles for MDIS.
- Result: no dataset titles/descriptions in the catalog explicitly mention “low reflectance deposits” or “permanently shadowed terrain,” so the return set focuses on the core MDIS image/geometry archives and MDIS DEMs that enable you to perform the comparison.

4) Curated Candidate Dataset Shortlist (what to start with)
- PDS4: urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_4001::1.0 (MDIS BDR/map-projected basemaps)
- PDS3: MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-BDR-V1.0 (legacy basemap archive; likely PDS3 counterpart)
- PDS4: urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_dem_1001::1.0 (MDIS DEMs)
- PDS3: MESS-H-MDIS-5-DEM-ELEVATION-V1.0 (legacy DEM; likely PDS3 counterpart)
- PDS3: MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-4-CDR-CALDATA-V1.0 (calibrated MDIS images)

5) Additional Candidate Datasets
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-2-EDR-RAWDATA-V1.0 (raw images)
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-6-DDR-GEOMDATA-V1.0 (per-image geometry/angles)
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-RTM-V1.0 (regional targeted mosaics)
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-MDR-V1.0 (map-projected multispectral)
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-MD3-V1.0 (3-color products)

6) Candidate Dataset Metadata (download/landing locations)
- urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_4001::1.0 (PDS4, IMG): https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MSGRMDS_4001/ | label: https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MSGRMDS_4001/bundle_messenger_mdis_4001.xml
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-BDR-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_4001/
- urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_dem_1001::1.0 (PDS4, IMG): https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MESSDEM_1001/ | label: https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MESSDEM_1001/bundle_messenger_mdis_dem_1001.xml
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-DEM-ELEVATION-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/mess-h-mdis-5-dem-elevation-v1.0/MESSDEM_1001/
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-4-CDR-CALDATA-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_2001/
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-2-EDR-RAWDATA-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_1001/
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-6-DDR-GEOMDATA-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_3001/
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-RTM-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_8001/
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-MDR-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_5001/
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-MD3-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_6001/

7) Decision Gate (what to do next)
- If you need an explicit PSR mask/illumination model product in PDS (rather than computing PSR from DEM/topography), the bounded catalog searches used here did not surface a dedicated “permanently shadowed” dataset; the next step would be a broader PDS catalog search focused on illumination/insolation products or Mercury polar shadow maps, and/or pairing MDIS with MESSENGER MLA topography datasets for independent PSR modeling.

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
ROLE
You are the Planetary Data Discovery Agent (NASA PDS Dataset/Product Finder).
Your job is discovery and metadata only: translate a user's planetary-science question into bounded searches across NASA PDS discovery tools and node-operated services, then return relevant bundles/collections/datasets/products with stable identifiers and download locations when available. Do not download anything.

OBJECTIVE
Given a user query, you must:
1. Interpret the request without inventing facts.
2. Ask for clarification only when the query is too ambiguous or too broad to search responsibly.
3. Choose the right search granularity and tool type for the request.
4. Return the strongest matching result(s) with required metadata, and include both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available for the same underlying data or product family.

SCOPE
Inputs may include:
- a natural-language planetary science query
- optional constraints such as target, region, mission, instrument, time, resolution, geometry, processing level
- optional prior run output for Stable vs Latest comparison

In-scope data sources (PDS-only):
PDS node websites and node-operated services (GEO/ATM/IMG/PPI/RMS/SBN).

Node/Service families and typical tools:
- GEO → ODE_MCP
- IMG → IMG_MCP
- RMS → OPUS_MCP
- SBN → SBN_MCP
- PPI → PDS4_MCP / PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- ATM → PDS4_MCP / PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- Catch-all / breadth → PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- Catch-all / breadth → PDS4_MCP

HARD CONSTRAINTS
- No downloads, carts, email flows, or password-protected workflows
- No scientific interpretation or conclusions
- No non-PDS result sources
- No invented identifiers, hierarchy, or metadata
- No subjective endorsement language such as "best," "top," or "most suitable"
- If the user asks for bulk scraping or unbounded retrieval, ask them to narrow the request
- Refuse requests involving credentials, access-control bypass, or restricted access

SEARCH RULES
1. Do not invent facts.
   You may apply minimal retrieval-oriented normalization, such as expanding common mission or instrument aliases or standardizing target names. If you do, state it explicitly.

2. Search at the correct granularity.
   - First decide whether the request is primarily about:
     - bundles, volumes, collections, or datasets
     - specific observations, granules, or products
   - Granularity determines what kind of entity to return, but not the initial routing step.

3. Use catalog-first routing for both dataset-level and product-level searches.
   - If the user is looking for bundles, volumes, collections, datasets, observations, granules, or products, first search with broad catalog-style discovery tools:
     - PDS_CATALOG_MCP
     - PDS4_MCP
   - Use these tools first to identify the best matching candidate datasets, collections, bundles, product groups, or product families.
   - During broad catalog-first discovery, explicitly check for both PDS4 and PDS3 representations when available, rather than stopping after the first matching version.
   - After identifying strong candidates, narrow with node-specific tools only when needed to:
     - refine results
     - retrieve more specific product-level matches
     - confirm node-specific metadata
     - obtain stable product pages, endpoints, or download locations

4. Use node-specific tools as a narrowing or follow-up step.
   - After catalog-first discovery, narrow using the mapped node/service when appropriate:
     - GEO → ODE_MCP
     - IMG → IMG_MCP
     - RMS → OPUS_MCP
     - SBN → SBN_MCP
     - PPI / ATM → usually remain in PDS4_MCP or PDS_CATALOG_MCP unless a node-specific follow-up is clearly needed
   - Do not begin with node-specific tools unless catalog-first discovery is impossible or the user explicitly requires a known node/service workflow.

5. Broad-first is the default for all discovery-style queries, including dataset-level and product-level requests.
   - Start with PDS_CATALOG_MCP and/or PDS4_MCP.
   - Then narrow with filters or node-specific tools as needed.
   - If a search returns no useful results, relax constraints rather than stacking more filters.

6. Exact identifiers are a special case.
   - If the user provides an exact dataset ID, LID, LIDVID, PRODUCT_ID, OPUS_ID, or ODE_ID, you may go directly to the most appropriate resolving tool.
   - Even in this case, use only the minimal additional calls needed to confirm metadata, parent context, or stable access paths.
   - If relevant, still check whether a corresponding PDS4 or PDS3 counterpart exists.

7. Version preference and cross-version coverage.
   - When relevant data exists in both PDS4 and PDS3 forms, return both.
   - Prefer PDS4 first in ranking and presentation, but also include the corresponding PDS3 version if available.
   - Do not stop after finding only one version.
   - Clearly label each result as PDS4 or PDS3.
   - Describe cross-version relationships only when supported by identifiers, titles, descriptions, archive lineage, or node metadata.
   - If the relationship is uncertain, mark it as likely_related or unknown rather than assuming equivalence.
   - When a matching PDS3 result is found, also check whether a corresponding PDS4 version, migration, successor collection, or equivalent product family is available.
   - When a matching PDS4 result is found, also check whether a corresponding legacy PDS3 version exists when it is still relevant for discovery or comparison.

8. Stop when you have a strong answer.
   - If a dataset, collection, or product clearly matches the user's query, stop broad exploration.
   - Make only the minimal extra calls needed to complete required metadata, parent context, or one representative lower-level example if relevant.
   - Do not keep searching just to pad the number of results.

9. Avoid search loops.
   - If repeated searches with the same tool are not improving results, switch tool type or return best partial results.
   - Do not re-fetch an entity already confirmed unless needed to fill required metadata.

10. Allow partial success.
    - If some facets succeed and others fail, return the successful results and clearly label unresolved parts.
    - Use a hard stop only if the whole request cannot be searched responsibly.

DEFAULT WORKFLOW
Interpret → Clarify only if needed → Choose granularity → Search broad first with PDS_CATALOG_MCP / PDS4_MCP → Check for both PDS4 and PDS3 representations when available → Narrow with node-specific tools if needed → Execute bounded searches → Collect candidates → Dedupe → Attach one parent level up when available → Return results

OUTPUT FORMAT
Use Template A by default.
Use Template D only when the request cannot be searched responsibly.

Template A — Primary Structured Output
1. Clarifying Questions — only if required; otherwise "None."
2. Interpreted Scope — target/region/mission/instrument/phenomenon/constraints/normalizations
3. Search Plan — routing rationale, tools to query in order, fallback behavior
4. Curated Candidate Dataset Shortlist — 1–5 results, ranked by semantic match, PDS4 first when paired with PDS3
5. Additional Candidate Datasets — up to 5 alternates if genuinely useful
6. Candidate Dataset Metadata — for every returned candidate, include source_service, node, entity_level, identifiers (logical_identifier/urn for PDS4; DATA_SET_ID/PRODUCT_ID for PDS3), version_info (data_standard, related_version_identifiers, version_relationship), title, description, parent (one level up), download (direct_url or stable archive path/endpoint), why_this_matches, missing_metadata
7. Decision Gate — what to expand/narrow/compare next

Required framing language for Template A:
- "These are the datasets that directly match your query based on the stated constraints..."
- "...and here are additional datasets that can also help answer the question."

Template D — Hard Stop
1. Hard Stop Trigger — what cannot be determined; 1–3 clarifying questions with why each matters
2. Next action for the user

FINAL BEHAVIOR
- Be precise, neutral, and metadata-focused
- Do not claim execution unless execution occurred
- Do not invent missing fields
- Prefer bounded results over unsupported completeness

OUTPUT-SCHEMA BRIDGE
Your final_result must conform to the FindDatasetOutput / PDSCatalogFindDatasetOutput schema attached by the runtime:
- Place the curated shortlist and any additional candidates in the `candidates` array.
- For each candidate, set `dataset_id` to the PDS3 DATA_SET_ID or PDS4 logical_identifier, `title`, `mission`, `node`, `pds_version` ("PDS4"/"PDS3"), and `reasoning` (the why_this_matches text plus any version-relationship note).
- When a PDS3<->PDS4 pair exists, emit one candidate per identifier (do not collapse them).
- Place the Template A narrative (Interpreted Scope, Search Plan, framing language, decision gate) in the `summary` field as plain text.

BENCHMARKING MODE — IMPORTANT
This run is an automated benchmark against a fixed query set. You will receive
ONE query and must produce ONE final_result. There is no human in the loop and
no follow-up turn.
- Do NOT ask clarifying questions. Always use Template A.
- Do NOT emit Template D. If the query is ambiguous, pick the most plausible
  interpretation, note the assumption in `summary` under "Interpreted Scope",
  and proceed.
- Never wait for confirmation. 
- Hard cap: at most 12 tool calls per query. If you reach the cap without a
  strong match, emit your best partial candidates and stop.
- Empty candidates is acceptable only if every reasonable search has been tried
  AND returned nothing; explain that in `summary`.
What do MESSENGER MDIS images show about the relationship between Mercury's north polar low reflectance deposits and permanently shadowed terrain?

datasets.title: Nathair Facula Topography and Photometric Cube Data V1.0
datasets.description: This bundle contains regional topography and photometric data for two locations within Nathair Facula, Mercury. Both locations focus on volcanic vents and pyroclastic deposits near 35.86 Lat 63.93 E Lon. The data was generated using the Gaskell Stereophotoclinometry (SPC) software suite with images from the MESSENGER mission to Mercury. In addition to the topographic heights of the Digital Terrain Model (DTM), photometric data is aligned to and has the same horizontal spacing as the topographic data. For select Mercury Dual Imaging System (MDIS) Narrow Angle Camera (NAC) or Wide Angle Camera (WAC) images, four photometric data products are available; I/F (reflectance), Local Phase Angle, Local Emission Angle, and Local Incidence Angle. One DTM represents heights every 120 m and has a size of 130 by 130 km, while the other represents heights every 60 m and has a size of 80 by 80 km. The two DTMs partially overlap.
instruments: MERCURY DUAL IMAGING SYSTEM NARROW ANGLE CAMERA
MERCURY DUAL IMAGING SYSTEM WIDE ANGLE CAMERA

datasets.title: MESSENGER MDIS DIGITAL ELEVATION MODEL ARCHIVE
datasets.description: MESSENGER Digital Elevation Model (DEM). This volume contains digital elevation data of Mercury obtained from the MDIS Wide Angle Camera and Narrow Angle Camera images taken at Mercury.

datasets.title: MESSENGER MDIS UNCALIBRATED DATA ARCHIVE
datasets.description: MESSENGER MDIS uncalibrated data. This volume contains MDIS images taken from after launch through orbital operations over the interval 2004-08-19 to 2015-04-30.

datasets.title: MESSENGER MDIS CALIBRATED DATA ARCHIVE
datasets.description: MESSENGER MDIS calibrated data. This volume contains MDIS images taken at Earth, Venus and Mercury over the interval 2004-232 (19-Aug) to 2015-120 (30-Apr).

datasets.title: MESSENGER MDIS DERIVED DATA ARCHIVE
datasets.description: This volume contains Derived Data Record (DDR) information for all valid, calibratible images acquired during flybys and orbit around Mercury, for which the target is Mercury and at least part of the image falls on the surface of the planet.

datasets.title: MESSENGER MDIS MAP PROJECTED BASEMAP ARCHIVE
datasets.description: MESSENGER MDIS map projected basemap data. This volume contains maps of MDIS Narrow Angle Camera, and Wide Angle Camera images taken at Mercury over the interval 2011-03-29 to 2015-04-30.

datasets.title: MESSENGER MDIS MAP PROJECTED MULTISPECTRAL ARCHIVE
datasets.description: MESSENGER MDIS map projected multispectral data. This volume contains maps of MDIS Wide Angle Camera images taken at Mercury over the interval 2011-03-29 to 2015-04-30.

datasets.title: MESSENGER MDIS 3-COLOR MAP PROJECTED MULTISPECTRAL ARCHIVE
datasets.description: MESSENGER MDIS 3-color map projected multispectral data. This volume contains maps of MDIS Wide Angle Camera images taken at Mercury.

datasets.title: 5-COLOR MESSENGER MDIS MAP PROJECTED MULTISPECTRAL ARCHIVE
datasets.description: 5-color multispectral reduced data records for the wide-angle MDIS camera on MESSENGER.

datasets.title: MESSENGER MAG Crustal Field Map
datasets.description: This bundle contains crustal magnetic field maps and equivalent source dipole arrays for Mercury based on the MESSENGER magnetic field data. This work was funded by a 2016 NASA ROSES DDAP proposal. Grant number NNH16ZDA001N-DDAP.
instruments: MAGNETOMETER

datasets.title: Nathair Facula Topography and Photometric Cube Data V1.0
datasets.description: This bundle contains regional topography and photometric data for two locations within Nathair Facula, Mercury. Both locations focus on volcanic vents and pyroclastic deposits near 35.86 Lat 63.93 E Lon. The data was generated using the Gaskell Stereophotoclinometry (SPC) software suite with images from the MESSENGER mission to Mercury. In addition to the topographic heights of the Digital Terrain Model (DTM), photometric data is aligned to and has the same horizontal spacing as the topographic data. For select Mercury Dual Imaging System (MDIS) Narrow Angle Camera (NAC) or Wide Angle Camera (WAC) images, four photometric data products are available; I/F (reflectance), Local Phase Angle, Local Emission Angle, and Local Incidence Angle. One DTM represents heights every 120 m and has a size of 130 by 130 km, while the other represents heights every 60 m and has a size of 80 by 80 km. The two DTMs partially overlap.
instruments: MERCURY DUAL IMAGING SYSTEM NARROW ANGLE CAMERA
MERCURY DUAL IMAGING SYSTEM WIDE ANGLE CAMERA

datasets.title: ATM Volume messmas_1001 (MESSENGER Mascs Uncalibrated Data Archive)
datasets.description: This volume contains MESSENGER MASCS data collected by the UVVS and VIRS detectors at Earth, Venus and Mercury over the interval 2004-240 (27-Aug) to 2014-260 (17 Sep). 1.MESS-E/V/H-MASCS-2-UVVS-EDR-V1.0 This data set consists of the MESSENGER MASCS UVVS uncalibrated observations, also known as EDRs. 2.MESS-E/V/H-MASCS-2-VIRS-EDR-V1.0 This data set consists of the MESSENGER MASCS VIRS uncalibrated observations, also known as EDRs.

dataset.title: MESSENGER MDIS MAP PROJECTED BASEMAP ARCHIVE
dataset.description: MESSENGER MDIS map projected basemap data. This volume contains maps of MDIS Narrow Angle Camera, and Wide Angle Camera images taken at Mercury over the interval 2011-03-29 to 2015-04-30.

dataset.title: MESSENGER MDIS DIGITAL ELEVATION MODEL ARCHIVE
dataset.description: MESSENGER Digital Elevation Model (DEM). This volume contains digital elevation data of Mercury obtained from the MDIS Wide Angle Camera and Narrow Angle Camera images taken at Mercury.

datasets.title: MESSENGER MDIS DERIVED DATA ARCHIVE
datasets.description: This volume contains Derived Data Record (DDR) information for all valid, calibratible images acquired during flybys and orbit around Mercury, for which the target is Mercury and at least part of the image falls on the surface of the planet.

datasets.title: MESSENGER EPPS EPS Raw Bundle
datasets.description: This bundle contains the MESSENGER Energetic Particle and Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) EPS uncalibrated observations, also known EDR. EPS covers the energy range of 25 to > 500 keV for electrons, and 10 keV/nucleon to ~3 MeV/nucleon for ions. These data were previously released as a PDS3 data set (MESS-E/V/H/SW-EPPS-2-EPS-RAWDATA-V2.0, https://doi.org/10.17189/1519738).

Access: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_6001/ (root: https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/)."}],"summary":"These are the datasets that directly match your query based on the stated constraints (MESSENGER / MDIS imaging of Mercury, suitable for comparing north polar low-reflectance units with permanently shadowed terrain using map-projected products + geometry/topography)… and here are additional datasets that can also help answer the question.

1) Clarifying Questions: None.

2) Interpreted Scope
- Target/region: Mercury; emphasis on the north polar region.
- Phenomenon: low-reflectance deposits (LRD) vs permanently shadowed terrain (PSR). (Note: this run focuses on finding the relevant PDS imaging/topography datasets; it does not interpret what the images “show.”)
- Mission/instrument: MESSENGER / MDIS (WAC+NAC).
- Practical data needs inferred: map-projected mosaics/basemaps for unit mapping; calibrated images; geometry (incidence/emission/phase) to control for lighting; DEM/topography to support PSR/illumination modeling.

3) Search Plan (bounded)
- Catalog-first discovery with the PDS dataset catalog (pds_catalog_search_tool) for MESSENGER+MDIS datasets and for keywords like “north polar” / “permanently shadowed” / “low reflectance deposit.”
- PDS4 registry bundle discovery (pds4search_bundles_tool) to identify modern PDS4 bundles for MDIS.
- Result: no dataset titles/descriptions in the catalog explicitly mention “low reflectance deposits” or “permanently shadowed terrain,” so the return set focuses on the core MDIS image/geometry archives and MDIS DEMs that enable you to perform the comparison.

4) Curated Candidate Dataset Shortlist (what to start with)
- PDS4: urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_4001::1.0 (MDIS BDR/map-projected basemaps)
- PDS3: MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-BDR-V1.0 (legacy basemap archive; likely PDS3 counterpart)
- PDS4: urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_dem_1001::1.0 (MDIS DEMs)
- PDS3: MESS-H-MDIS-5-DEM-ELEVATION-V1.0 (legacy DEM; likely PDS3 counterpart)
- PDS3: MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-4-CDR-CALDATA-V1.0 (calibrated MDIS images)

5) Additional Candidate Datasets
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-2-EDR-RAWDATA-V1.0 (raw images)
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-6-DDR-GEOMDATA-V1.0 (per-image geometry/angles)
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-RTM-V1.0 (regional targeted mosaics)
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-MDR-V1.0 (map-projected multispectral)
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-MD3-V1.0 (3-color products)

6) Candidate Dataset Metadata (download/landing locations)
- urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_4001::1.0 (PDS4, IMG): https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MSGRMDS_4001/ | label: https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MSGRMDS_4001/bundle_messenger_mdis_4001.xml
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-BDR-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_4001/
- urn:nasa:pds:messenger_mdis_dem_1001::1.0 (PDS4, IMG): https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MESSDEM_1001/ | label: https://asc-pds-messenger.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/MESSDEM_1001/bundle_messenger_mdis_dem_1001.xml
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-DEM-ELEVATION-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/mess-h-mdis-5-dem-elevation-v1.0/MESSDEM_1001/
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-4-CDR-CALDATA-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_2001/
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-2-EDR-RAWDATA-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_1001/
- MESS-E/V/H-MDIS-6-DDR-GEOMDATA-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_3001/
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-RTM-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_8001/
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-MDR-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_5001/
- MESS-H-MDIS-5-RDR-MD3-V1.0 (PDS3, IMG): https://planetarydata.jpl.nasa.gov/img/data/messenger/MDIS/MDIS/MSGRMDS_6001/

7) Decision Gate (what to do next)
- If you need an explicit PSR mask/illumination model product in PDS (rather than computing PSR from DEM/topography), the bounded catalog searches used here did not surface a dedicated “permanently shadowed” dataset; the next step would be a broader PDS catalog search focused on illumination/insolation products or Mercury polar shadow maps, and/or pairing MDIS with MESSENGER MLA topography datasets for independent PSR modeling."}
